opp.name: HMH_S2P_Feb26
opp.next_step: Send detailed follow-up email with company overview, AI capabilities, Gartner report, and module details Coordinate and schedule a live demo/learning session with Judson (Ops Leader) Present full S2P suite including autonomous negotiation agent and AI-driven risk management Discuss commercial framework and SAP integration approach Reconnect within one month to track RFP progress
opp.ais_why:
• HMH procurement leadership shows strong curiosity around autonomous negotiation agents, automated risk monitoring, and ML-driven contract intelligence as part of their 2026 S2P RFP.
• Next steps explicitly call for presenting the full S2P suite including the autonomous negotiation agent and AI-driven risk management to Ops Leader Judson.
• Active Qualified stage with RFP planned this year; demo coordination underway with strong AI capability emphasis in follow-up plan.

sources: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson Brown: 'Inconsistencies across multiple manufacturing and service sites pose challenges, and the team lacks unified supplier risk visibility and maintainable integrations'
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson Brown: role clarity provided that he leads global Sourcing Operations and Process, aiming to standardize ERP and procurement processes across merged business divisions
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026: no quantified savings, cost reduction, or ROI discussed
metrics.narrative: Judson Brown (Global Sourcing Operations and Process lead) articulated operational pain on the 11 Mar 2026 call: inconsistencies across multiple manufacturing and service sites, lack of unified supplier risk visibility, and non-maintainable integrations. He stated the goal is to 'standardize ERP and procurement processes across merged business divisions.' The pain is confirmed and role-owned (Judson leads the standardization mandate), but no quantified business case was discussed—no cost of the current fragmentation, no headcount burden, no cycle-time penalty, no savings target, no ROI model. The call focused on capability fit (AI-driven RFP authoring, autonomous negotiation, supplier risk, contract management, spend analytics integrated with SAP and Teams) but stopped short of sizing the problem financially. To move this deal forward, the immediate action is to co-build a quantified value case with Judson: map the cost of manual RFP assembly, the supplier-data lag that disrupts MRP planning, and the compliance-screening gaps he flagged, and translate those into a defensible ROI that can secure budget and executive sponsorship.

sources: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026: Judson led 90-minute discovery, asked detailed integration and configurability questions, expressed interest in eSourcing, Supplier Risk, Contract Mgmt, and Spend Analytics
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'Will review all provided information and share it with the team and get their thoughts'
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026: no stakeholder introductions, no EB/DM identified, no competitor discussion, no advocacy demonstrated
106 days of silence since 12 Mar—no reply to follow-up, no demo scheduled, no RFP update
Salesforce tasks show Serhat Alpar (SVP Sourcing) was contacted earlier, but his involvement in THIS opp is unconfirmed
champion.narrative: Judson Brown is the only engaged contact and shows early champion potential but has not demonstrated advocacy or the ability to drive internal action. On the 11 Mar call he led the 90-minute discovery session, asked detailed technical and integration questions, expressed interest in the AI-driven eSourcing and Supplier Risk modules, and committed to 'review all provided information and share it with the team and get their thoughts.' He articulated the pain and the strategic mandate (post-merger standardization) clearly. However, 107 days later there has been zero follow-up—no reply to Bailey's planned follow-up materials, no demo scheduled, no stakeholder introductions, no RFP progress update. This suggests Judson is either (a) an influencer without the authority or internal sponsorship to move the deal forward, (b) facing internal roadblocks he has not surfaced to Zycus, or (c) deprioritized the initiative. He has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or broader buying committee (IT, Finance, Procurement leadership), and he did not push back on competitors (none were discussed) or advocate for Zycus over alternatives. His engagement was active on 11 Mar but has since cooled to silent. Current status: developing champion at risk of fading. Next move: re-engage Judson with a direct, value-focused message—'Judson, it's been 3.5 months since we walked through the S2P suite and you were going to loop in your team. What's blocking progress, and can we set up a 15-minute call this week to get the evaluation back on track? Happy to bring in our VP to discuss exec-level sponsorship if that helps.' If no response within 5 business days, escalate to Serhat Alpar (SVP Sourcing, who was cc'd on initial outreach per task notes) and ask for his guidance on how to re-engage the HMH procurement leadership team.

sources: Avoma calls 19 Feb and 11 Mar 2026: no competitors named, no RFP shortlist discussed, no vendor comparison mentioned
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Bailey: 'do you foresee you guys going the RFP route'—no clear answer from Judson
SF Competitors__c field: empty
Inference: 106-day silence after engaged discovery suggests possible silent competition, SAP-native alternative consideration, or do-nothing scenario
competition.narrative: No competitors were named or discussed on either the 19 Feb or 11 Mar calls. Judson did not mention evaluating other vendors, running an RFP shortlist, or comparing Zycus to alternatives. Bailey asked on the 11 Mar call 'do you foresee you guys going the RFP route' but did not probe whether HMH is already talking to Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, or other S2P platforms. The homegrown supplier portal Judson described is a manual, low-capability internal tool (Excel/Word document assembly for RFPs), not a commercial competitor. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. However, the 106-day silence and lack of urgency could indicate HMH is either (a) running a quiet, broad RFP process Judson has not disclosed, (b) evaluating SAP-native Ariba modules as a lower-effort path given their existing SAP ERP footprint (Judson's question about whether Zycus 'overlays' SAP or replaces it hints at this concern), or (c) deprioritizing procurement transformation entirely (do-nothing is the competitor). For a deal that has been dark for 15+ weeks after an engaged discovery call, the most likely explanations are either silent competition or loss of internal sponsorship. Next move: when re-engaging Judson, ask directly—'Are you evaluating other platforms alongside Zycus, and if so, where do we stand relative to them?'—and probe whether HMH's IT or Finance teams are pushing a different direction (e.g., SAP-native, or delay). If Judson confirms this is sole-sourced and the only block is internal prioritization, the action shifts to securing executive sponsorship (COO or CFO) to resource the initiative. If he reveals a competitive process, the action is to run a deep discovery on decision criteria and position Zycus's AI and integration strengths as differentiators.

sources: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'Inconsistencies across multiple manufacturing and service sites pose challenges, and the team lacks unified supplier risk visibility and maintainable integrations'

Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'The current supplier onboarding management process needs improvement to enhance integration with supplier risk and performance management, particularly in compliance screening and financial checks'
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: homegrown supplier portal for RFPs requires manual Excel/Word document assembly and upload; no in-system authoring
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: Concur eInvoicing 'is not a lot, and so I run into' workflow friction
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: role clarity provided that he leads global Sourcing Operations and Process, aiming to standardize ERP and procurement processes across merged business divisions
identify_pain.narrative: Judson Brown owns and clearly articulated the operational pain on the 11 Mar 2026 call. HMH has merged multiple business divisions and now operates 'multiple manufacturing and service sites' with 'inconsistencies' in procurement and ERP processes. The current state: SAP ERP for procurement and MRP, Concur for eInvoicing (which Judson flagged as low-configurability and friction-prone), a homegrown supplier portal for RFPs and technical queries (requiring manual Excel/Word document assembly and upload, no in-system authoring), and a PLM system for engineering documentation. Judson stated the team 'lacks unified supplier risk visibility and maintainable integrations,' and the 'current supplier onboarding management process needs improvement to enhance integration with supplier risk and performance management, particularly in compliance screening and financial checks.' He also flagged that 'there's not a lot of configurability, and so I run into' workflow and follow-up issues. The pain is CONFIRMED, role-owned (Judson leads the standardization mandate), and tied to a strategic initiative (post-merger ERP and procurement process standardization). However, the pain was articulated 107 days ago and has not been re-confirmed or escalated since—no follow-up call, no stakeholder validation, no urgency signal. The 106-day silence suggests either the pain is not acute enough to drive immediate action, or Judson lacks the authority/sponsorship to act on it. Next move: re-engage and validate whether the pain is still live—'Is standardizing supplier risk and procurement processes still a priority for HMH in 2026, and what's blocking progress?'—and escalate to the executive sponsor (likely COO or CFO) who can resource and fund the fix.

sources: Avoma calls 19 Feb and 11 Mar 2026: no discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement policy, or partner involvement
Account contact Troy Matherne (CIO) not engaged in this opp
No Tasks or Events logged related to security questionnaire, legal review, or contract draft
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and SI/partner involvement were not discussed on either the 19 Feb or 11 Mar calls. No MSA, SOW, or Order Form steps were mentioned. Judson's questions focused entirely on functional fit and SAP integration; he did not surface security-review requirements, legal redline expectations, or procurement-policy gates (e.g., whether HMH requires a formal RFP response, references, or SOC2/ISO certification before contracting). The account contact list includes Troy Matherne (CIO), who would typically own InfoSec and IT compliance sign-off for a platform purchase, but he has not been engaged in this deal. For a $150k suite deployment across merged business divisions and multiple manufacturing sites, HMH will almost certainly require InfoSec review, ERP integration scoping (which Judson asked about but was not detailed), and likely a formal procurement process given the cross-functional impact. The 106-day silence may indicate the deal is stalled behind an internal process Judson cannot navigate alone. Next step: when re-engaging, ask Judson explicitly—'What legal, security, and procurement steps does HMH require before we can finalize a contract, and who owns each gate?'—and request an intro to the relevant stakeholder (CIO) and the procurement/legal leads to map the paper process in parallel with the business-case build.

SF Opportunity has zero buyer-side contact roles logged
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. The single engaged contact is Judson Brown, an operations and process lead with no budget authority signaled on the call. He did not discuss pricing, commercial terms, or approval chains, and no executive (CFO, COO, CPO) appeared on either the 19 Feb or 11 Mar calls. The account-level contact list includes Tom McGee (CFO), Chuck Chauviere (COO), and Serhat Alpar (SVP Sourcing), but none are linked to this opportunity via contact roles or call attendance, so their involvement in THIS deal's budget decision is unconfirmed. The 11 Mar call notes show Judson will 'share with the team and get their thoughts,' implying he is an influencer gathering input, not the commercial decision-maker. For a $150k full-suite S2P deal, budget authority almost certainly sits with the CFO or COO. The immediate action is to ask Judson directly: 'Who controls the budget and commercial approval for a platform investment of this scope, and can we bring them into the next conversation to validate the business case and timeline?' Without EB engagement this deal cannot progress past Qualified, and the 106-day silence suggests Judson may lack the authority or internal sponsorship to drive it forward alone.

sources: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'Will review all provided information to inform the decision-making process' and 'share it with the team and get their thoughts'
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026: Bailey asked 'do you foresee you guys going the RFP route, or is this something you guys would wanna learn more about'—no clear answer from Judson
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026: proposed 'follow-up check-in in about a month' (mid-April target, now 10+ weeks overdue)
Call attendance: only Judson present on 11 Mar, no IT/Finance/exec escalation, no cross-functional committee
106-day silence since 12 Mar with no process update
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is not yet formalized. Judson Brown mentioned on the 11 Mar call that he would 'review all provided information, including demo videos, marketing assets, and customer white papers, to inform the decision-making process' and 'share it with the team and get their thoughts,' indicating an internal evaluation phase follows the initial discovery. Bailey proposed a 'follow-up check-in scheduled in about a month' (which would have been mid-April, now 10+ weeks overdue). No RFP timeline, vendor-selection stages, or approval gates were discussed. The call sequence itself shows early-stage discovery: a brief 19 Feb intro call, then a 90-minute 11 Mar capability walk-through with Judson alone—no IT, Finance, or executive participation, signaling the deal has not yet escalated to a cross-functional buying committee. Judson asked whether HMH would 'go the RFP route' and Bailey's response was 'do you foresee you guys going the RFP route, or is this something you guys would wanna learn more about,' leaving the process undefined. The 106-day silence since suggests either (a) Judson is stuck building internal consensus and has no clear process, or (b) HMH paused the evaluation without telling Zycus. Next move: re-engage Judson and ask for a concrete process map—'What are the next gates, who needs to sign off, and what's the realistic timeline to run an RFP or pilot and reach a decision?'—and insist on meeting the broader stakeholder group (IT, Finance, Procurement leadership) to confirm this is a live, funded initiative with executive sponsorship.

sources: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'Interest was expressed in Zycus eSourcing (AI-driven RFP authoring and autonomous negotiation), Supplier Information & Risk Management, Contract Management, and Spend Analytics, all integrated with SAP and Teams'
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'how well can you integrate other data and set rules and configure follow-ups' (configurability as criterion)
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'Would these overlay merge and mesh with SAP, or is it you switch over from an SAP-generated RFP into the eSourcing module' (integration approach as criterion)
Avoma call 11 Mar 2026, Judson: 'there's not a lot, and so I run into ' (pain point driving criterion)
decision_criteria.narrative: Judson Brown outlined evaluation priorities on the 11 Mar 2026 call: AI-driven RFP authoring and autonomous negotiation (eSourcing), Supplier Information & Risk Management (addressing compliance screening and financial checks gaps), Contract Management (visibility and expiration alerts), Spend Analytics, and integration with the relevant stakeholder and Microsoft Teams. He emphasized 'configurability' as a key concern, asking 'how well can you integrate other data and set rules and configure follow-ups' in the eInvoicing module, and flagged that their current Concur setup 'is not a lot ' and causes friction. He also asked whether Zycus overlays SAP or replaces SAP-native procurement functions ('does this merge and mesh with those, or is it you switch over from an SAP-generated RFP into the eSourcing module'), signaling integration approach as a decision criterion. Bailey walked through the suite but no formal scorecard, weighted criteria, or RFP rubric was discussed, and no other stakeholders (IT, Finance, Procurement leadership) were present to confirm enterprise-wide decision criteria. The criteria are inferred from Judson's questions and pain points, not from a documented BRD or multi-stakeholder requirements session. Next step: ask Judson to share the actual RFP or evaluation framework the broader team will use, and confirm whether IT (CIO Troy Matherne) and Finance (CFO Tom McGee) will add security, cost, and ERP-integration criteria beyond what Judson surfaced.

He showed interest in AI-driven RFP authoring and autonomous negotiation agent. No AIS score logged in Salesforce. Based on call evidence, HMH falls into AI Curious tier—open to AI capabilities but not leading with AI as a forcing function or decision criterion. The AI interest has not been converted into urgency or a quantified value case.

No champion, no multi-threading, no advancement from Qualified in 127 days. Ghost pattern confirms deprioritization.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is off track after 106 days of silence. Immediate actions: re-engage Judson with urgency-driven message and escalate to HMH executive sponsor. Build quantified ROI case and multi-thread beyond single contact.

competitive_position.summary: No competitors identified in any source (Salesforce fields, Avoma calls, tasks, next steps). Judson did not mention evaluating other vendors or running an RFP shortlist on the 11 Mar call. However, the 106-day silence after an engaged discovery session raises three possibilities: (1) HMH is running a quiet, undisclosed competitive evaluation; (2) IT/Finance is pushing SAP-native Ariba modules as a lower-effort alternative given HMH's existing SAP ERP footprint (Judson asked whether Zycus 'overlays' SAP or replaces SAP functions, signaling integration approach as a concern); (3) do-nothing is the real competitor—HMH has deprioritized procurement transformation and Judson lacks the sponsorship to drive it. The absence of disclosed competition combined with prolonged silence suggests either competitive pressure Judson has not surfaced or loss of internal momentum. Most likely current threat: do-nothing / deprioritization, given zero follow-up in 15+ weeks and no executive engagement.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Moderate interest expressed but not converted to urgency
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI interest expressed by Judson on 11 Mar call—he asked about Zycus AI models ('what models do you guys use for the AI?') and Bailey walked through AI-driven RFP authoring, autonomous negotiation agent (Merlin), and supplier risk management. Judson specifically asked for details on the 'internal AI model used in Zycus platform' (built with GE). However, the AI story was presented in capability-overview mode, not tied to Judson's specific pain (manual RFP assembly, compliance-screening gaps) or quantified business impact. The 106-day silence suggests the AI pitch did not create urgency or differentiation strong enough to drive immediate action. Under-positioned: the Merlin negotiation agent and AI-driven risk management are relevant to HMH's post-merger standardization mandate but were not anchored to a compelling business case or competitive wedge.

items.quote: Interest was expressed in Zycus eSourcing (AI-driven RFP authoring and autonomous negotiation)
items.criterion: AI-driven RFP authoring and autonomous negotiation (eSourcing)

items.quote: The current supplier onboarding management process needs improvement to enhance integration with supplier risk and performance management, particularly in compliance screening and financial checks
items.criterion: Supplier Information & Risk Management (unified visibility, compliance screening, financial checks)

items.quote: Interest expressed in Contract Management; Judson noted need for notification system to alert team when contracts approaching expiration
Judson asked: 'Would these overlay merge and mesh with SAP, or is it you switch over from an SAP-generated RFP into the eSourcing module?' — integration approach is a decision criterion and concern
items.criterion: SAP ERP integration and Microsoft Teams integration

items.quote: Judson asked: 'how well can you integrate other data and set rules and configure follow-ups' in eInvoicing; flagged current Concur setup 'is not a lot ' and causes friction
No quantified savings, cost reduction, headcount impact, or ROI discussed on 11 Mar call
Only Judson present on 11 Mar call; no IT (CIO Troy Matherne), Finance (CFO Tom McGee), or Procurement exec (SVP Serhat Alpar) engagement; Judson is Ops lead, not commercial decision-maker
items.source: Avoma call 11 Mar 2026 attendance; SF contact roles (zero buyer roles logged)
items.criterion: Executive and cross-functional validation (IT, Finance, Procurement leadership)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional alignment on the 11 Mar discovery call—Judson's pain points (procurement fragmentation, lack of supplier risk visibility, low configurability in current tools, manual RFP assembly) map directly to Zycus's S2P suite strengths (AI-driven eSourcing, Supplier Risk, Contract Mgmt, Spend Analytics, SAP/Teams integration). However, three critical gaps expose the deal: (1) no quantified business case or ROI to justify $150k investment and secure budget; (2) integration approach (overlay vs. replace SAP-native functions) is unresolved and a stated concern for Judson; (3) no executive or cross-functional validation—Judson is an Ops lead, not the commercial decision-maker, and IT/Finance/Procurement leadership have not weighed in on fit or feasibility.

hard.ais_why:
• HMH procurement leadership shows strong curiosity around autonomous negotiation agents, automated risk monitoring, and ML-driven contract intelligence as part of their 2026 S2P RFP.
• Next steps explicitly call for presenting the full S2P suite including the autonomous negotiation agent and AI-driven risk management to Ops Leader Judson.
• Active Qualified stage with RFP planned this year; demo coordination underway with strong AI capability emphasis in follow-up plan.
hard.next_step:
• Send detailed follow-up email with company overview, AI capabilities, Gartner report, and module details

  • Coordinate and schedule a live demo/learning session with Judson (Ops Leader)

  • Present full S2P suite including autonomous negotiation agent and AI-driven risk management

  • Discuss commercial framework and SAP integration approach

  • Reconnect within one month to track RFP progress

pulse.summary: Dark: no verified buyer engagement in 106 day(s) (last 2026-03-12).
